title: US and Iran in blockade standoff as Pakistan pushes for talks
summary_cn: 美国总统特朗普在社交媒体宣布延长停火，但伊朗和美国在霍尔木兹海峡展开封锁对抗，局势紧张。巴基斯坦等待和谈代表，但伊朗代表团因美方行为矛盾而迟迟未到，外交努力面临挑战。
paragraphs_en: Courtesy of last night's Truth Social post from US President Donald Trump, the ceasefire between Iran, the US and Israel which was due to expire on Wednesday does at least persist.
Instead of fighting, we have a "war of blockades" over the Strait of Hormuz, with both sides using force to intercept and seize commercial vessels.
The mood out in one of the world's most important waterways is combustible. It would be unwise to bet against events spiralling out of control.
In the meantime, Islamabad still waits for Iranian and American representatives to arrive for peace talks.
Parts of the city remain sealed off, the signs are still up and the hotel where talks were expected to take place is empty, ready for the hoped-for return of high-level delegations.
But after several days of fevered anticipation, the atmosphere has changed.

Gone is the talk of press pools in faraway Washington being told to head for the airport, or speculation about the contents of the giant C-17 Globemaster transport planes that landed at a nearby military airbase earlier in the week.

In its place is the gloomy realisation that an opportunity for Pakistan to prove itself on the international stage, to broker a deal - any kind of deal - between mortal enemies may have slipped out of Islamabad's grasp. For now.

Pakistan has not given up. Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif, who has invested considerable diplomatic capital in getting the two parties together, posted on social media that Pakistan would "continue its earnest efforts for negotiated settlement of the conflict".
Donald Trump has told at least one journalist that a deal is still possible in the next few days.

It's hard to know if this is reality speaking, or the voice of an impatient man, anxious to remove Iran from his most urgent to-do list before King Charles arrives in Washington for a state visit next Monday - and Trump's much anticipated visit to China not long afterwards.

Iran dismissed the president's suggestion that he was giving Tehran time to come up with a "unified position", but it seems unlikely that the regime, already bruised and battered by the war, will break the ceasefire, thus inviting more punishment from the air.
In the meantime, what are we to make of the Iranian delegation's reluctance to get on a plane for the short ride to Islamabad?
Iran accuses the US of a "breach of commitments" and cites what it called Washington's "contradictory behaviour".

Donald Trump's wildly conflicting public pronouncements – threatening apocalyptic punishment one moment and offering an olive branch the next, claiming all the while that Iran has already made significant concessions – have muddied the waters.
Iran still complains that it has entered negotiations twice in the past year, only to be attacked by Israel and the United States.
But Donald Trump's Truth Social post announcing the ceasefire extension, lacking its usual bombast, spoke of an Iranian regime that is "seriously fractured, not unexpectedly".
For a man who has trumpeted the fact that he has already achieved regime change in Iran, was this an admission that Washington is struggling to know who it's dealing with?
Has "regime fracture" made the business of diplomacy with Iran – never the easiest art to master – that much more difficult?

Deliberately or not, the president, through his choice of words, briefly joined a debate that has been raging among seasoned Iran watchers in recent days: who's in charge in Iran now that so much of the old leadership is gone?
paragraphs_cn: 得益于美国总统唐纳德·特朗普（Donald Trump）昨晚在Truth Social上的帖子，伊朗、美国和以色列之间原定于周三到期的停火协议至少得以延续。
双方没有直接交战，而是在霍尔木兹海峡（Strait of Hormuz）展开了一场“封锁战”，彼此都动用武力拦截并扣押商业船只。
这条全球最重要的水道之一气氛紧张，一触即发。赌局势不会失控是不明智的。
与此同时，伊斯兰堡（Islamabad）仍在等待伊朗和美国代表抵达进行和平谈判。
城市的部分区域仍然封锁，警示标志依然矗立，原定举行会谈的酒店空无一人，只为等待高级别代表团有望归来。
但经过几天的热切期待后，气氛已经变了。
不再有关于远在华盛顿（Washington）的记者团被告知前往机场的讨论，也不再猜测本周早些时候降落在附近军事基地的巨型C-17环球霸王运输机（C-17 Globemaster transport planes）里装着什么。
取而代之的是一种阴郁的认识：巴基斯坦（Pakistan）在国际舞台上证明自己、在死敌之间斡旋达成协议——任何协议——的机会可能已经从伊斯兰堡手中溜走了。至少目前如此。
巴基斯坦并未放弃。总理谢赫巴兹·谢里夫（Shehbaz Sharif）为促成双方会面投入了大量外交资本，他在社交媒体上发文称，巴基斯坦将“继续为通过谈判解决冲突做出真诚努力”。
唐纳德·特朗普已告诉至少一名记者，未来几天仍有可能达成协议。
很难判断这是现实情况，还是一个不耐烦之人的声音——他急于在下周一查尔斯国王（King Charles）抵达华盛顿进行国事访问（以及不久后特朗普备受期待的访华行程）之前，将伊朗从他最紧迫的待办事项清单上划掉。
伊朗驳斥了总统关于他给德黑兰（Tehran）时间提出“统一立场”的说法，但看起来，这个已在战争中伤痕累累的政权不太可能打破停火，从而招致更多空中打击。
与此同时，我们该如何看待伊朗代表团不愿登上飞机、短途飞往伊斯兰堡的态度？
伊朗指责美国“违反承诺”，并提及它所谓的华盛顿“自相矛盾的行为”。
唐纳德·特朗普公开言论的极度矛盾——时而威胁毁灭性惩罚，时而又伸出橄榄枝，同时一直声称伊朗已做出重大让步——让局势更加混乱。
伊朗仍抱怨说，过去一年它已两次进入谈判，却遭到以色列和美国的攻击。
但唐纳德·特朗普在Truth Social上宣布延长停火的帖子，一反往常的浮夸，提到了一个“严重分裂，这并不意外”的伊朗政权。
对于一个曾大肆宣扬自己已实现伊朗政权更迭的人来说，这是否承认了华盛顿正难以弄清它在与谁打交道？
“政权分裂”是否让与伊朗的外交——这门从来就不易掌握的艺术——变得更加困难？
无论有意无意，总统通过他的措辞，短暂地加入了近日资深伊朗观察家们激烈辩论的话题：既然那么多旧领导层已不复存在，现在伊朗谁在掌权？

keywords.definition_en: A temporary suspension of fighting, typically during a war.
keywords.context_sentence: Courtesy of last night's Truth Social post from US President Donald Trump, the ceasefire between Iran, the US and Israel which was due to expire on Wednesday does at least persist.
keywords.context_translation: 多亏了昨晚美国总统唐纳德·特朗普在Truth Social上的帖子，伊朗、美国和以色列之间原定于周三到期的停火协议至少得以延续。

keywords.definition_en: An act of sealing off a place to prevent goods or people from entering or leaving.
keywords.context_sentence: Instead of fighting, we have a 'war of blockades' over the Strait of Hormuz, with both sides using force to intercept and seize commercial vessels.
keywords.context_translation: 双方没有直接交战，而是在霍尔木兹海峡展开了一场'封锁战'，都使用武力拦截和扣押商船。

keywords.definition_en: Easily ignited and capable of burning rapidly; also used metaphorically for volatile situations.
keywords.context_sentence: The mood out in one of the world's most important waterways is combustible.
keywords.context_translation: 在这条世界最重要的水道之一，气氛一触即发。

keywords.definition_en: To arrange or negotiate a deal or agreement between parties.
keywords.context_sentence: In its place is the gloomy realisation that an opportunity for Pakistan to prove itself on the international stage, to broker a deal - any kind of deal - between mortal enemies may have slipped out of Islamabad's grasp.
keywords.context_translation: 取而代之的是，人们沮丧地意识到，巴基斯坦在国际舞台上证明自己、在死敌之间促成一项协议——任何协议——的机会可能已经从伊斯兰堡手中溜走了。

keywords.definition_en: A group of people sent to represent others, especially in negotiations.
keywords.context_sentence: Parts of the city remain sealed off, the signs are still up and the hotel where talks were expected to take place is empty, ready for the hoped-for return of high-level delegations.
keywords.context_translation: 城市的部分地区仍然封锁，标志还在，预计举行会谈的酒店空无一人，等待着高级代表团的回归。

keywords.definition_en: The forming of a theory or conjecture without firm evidence.
keywords.context_sentence: Gone is the talk of press pools in faraway Washington being told to head for the airport, or speculation about the contents of the giant C-17 Globemaster transport planes that landed at a nearby military airbase earlier in the week.
keywords.context_translation: 不再有关于远在华盛顿的记者团被告知前往机场的讨论，也没有了对本周早些时候降落在附近军事基地的巨型C-17环球霸王运输机内容的猜测。

keywords.definition_en: Unwillingness or hesitation to do something.
keywords.context_sentence: In the meantime, what are we to make of the Iranian delegation's reluctance to get on a plane for the short ride to Islamabad?
keywords.context_translation: 与此同时，我们该如何理解伊朗代表团不愿登上飞机前往伊斯兰堡的短途行程？

keywords.definition_en: Broken or split into parts, often used metaphorically for divisions within a group.
keywords.context_sentence: But Donald Trump's Truth Social post announcing the ceasefire extension, lacking its usual bombast, spoke of an Iranian regime that is 'seriously fractured, not unexpectedly'.
keywords.context_translation: 但唐纳德·特朗普在Truth Social上宣布延长停火的帖子，缺少了往常的夸夸其谈，提到了一个'严重分裂，这并不意外'的伊朗政权。
